title: Unnecessary `WIDTHEXPAND` warning/error when the LHS is wider in a binary operation
body:
### Example

```sv
module m (
    input  [1:0] i1,
    i2,
    output [3:0] o
);
  assign o = i1 + i2;
endmodule
```

Compile using:

```sh
verilator --binary m.sv
```

### Current Output

```
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: m.sv:6:17: Operator ADD expects 4 bits on the LHS, but LHS's VARREF 'i1' generates 2 bits.
...
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: m.sv:6:17: Operator ADD expects 4 bits on the RHS, but RHS's VARREF 'i2' generates 2 bits.
...
%Error: Exiting due to 2 warning(s)
```

### Expected output

No warning/errors. I tried the example above on Edaplayground using all four available commercial simulators and they don't issue any warning/error.

### Workaround

```
/* verilator lint_off WIDTHEXPAND */
...
/* verilator lint_on WIDTHEXPAND */
```
### Version

verilator 5.024 on Archlinux
